title: Berry-Curvature Trace Equals Projector Commutator
summary: For a smooth occupied-state frame U and projector P = U U dagger, the traced Berry curvature satisfies Tr F_12 = i Tr(P[partial_1 P, partial_2 P]), giving the local identity behind the TKNN projector formula.
aliases: trace of Berry curvature equals the projector commutator density
assumptions: A smooth local orthonormal occupied-state frame exists on the patch under discussion.
The occupied projector has constant rank on the patch.
regime: Local differential-geometric rewrite inside the TKNN band invariant.
scope: Archival TKNN bridge between Berry-connection notation and the occupied-projector integrand.

source_anchors.notes: Archival geometric identity linking the Hall-conductance integrand to occupied-bundle curvature.
Witten states the Hall integer as the first Chern class of the filled-band bundle.

representation: Local TKNN identity making the projector commutator and the traced Berry curvature interchangeable.
motivation: This note exists because the TKNN formula is often quoted either in Berry-curvature language or in projector language, and the equivalence between those two expressions is a real intermediate lemma rather than a slogan.
prerequisite_story: Know the occupied-state bundle, the non-abelian Berry connection of a local orthonormal frame, and the algebraic identities satisfied by an orthogonal projector P with P^2 = P.
local_context_story: Inside the Lecture Two Hall-response branch, this is the archival TKNN bridge that turns Witten's first-Chern-class statement into the explicit occupied-projector density used in microscopic Hall-conductance formulas.
forward_links_story: Once this identity is established, the Brillouin-zone integral of Tr F can be rewritten as the projector formula for k', and then the band invariant can be matched to the many-body twist-angle invariant.
canonical_questions: Why does the traced non-abelian curvature reduce to i Tr(P dP wedge dP)?
Which algebraic identity from P^2 = P kills the unwanted terms?
Why is this only a local frame computation even though the final Chern number is global?
scope_boundaries: This is a local bundle identity on a smooth patch with constant occupied-band rank; global integrality is a separate step.
The lemma concerns the traced curvature of the occupied bundle, not an arbitrary matrix-valued connection.

math_expressions.notes: Occupied frame and projector on a local patch.
Berry connection and curvature in a local occupied frame.
Identity relating the traced curvature density to the projector commutator.

symbols.meaning: matrix whose columns are a local orthonormal occupied-state frame
non-abelian Berry-curvature two-form of the occupied bundle

step_justification: Differentiate U dagger U equals 1 to relate dU dagger U and U dagger dU.
Use P = UU dagger and P squared equals P to rewrite dP in terms of transitions between occupied and empty subspaces.
Expand Tr F in the local frame and collapse the result to i Tr(P dP wedge dP).
admissible_assumptions: Work on a smooth local patch with a finite-rank occupied subbundle and a local orthonormal frame U.
Use standard matrix-valued differential-form identities instead of rederiving them from first principles in this lemma.
formalization_blockers: The Lean-side interface for matrix-valued differential forms and wedge-product trace identities is still missing.
The passage between local frame notation U and the abstract occupied bundle is not yet packaged as a reusable formal equivalence.
retrieval_hints: Use when a derivation must show exactly how the Berry-curvature language becomes the projector formula.
